# TestPilot Agent Workflow

<!-- Generated by TestPilot. Keep this marker to allow `testpilot init` to refresh this file. -->

Configured integrations: Claude Code, Codex.

When the user asks for a TestPilot workflow label, run the backing CLI command:

| Workflow label | Backing CLI command | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `test:new` | `testpilot new <name> --requirement <path>` | Create a test proposal workspace from a requirement document. |
| `test:analysis` | `testpilot analysis [name]` | Decompose requirements into testable items, risks, and questions. |
| `test:points` | `testpilot points [name]` | Generate core scenario test points for a test change. |
| `test:excel` | `testpilot excel [name]` | Export executable Excel test cases. |
| `test:mind` | `testpilot mind [name]` | Export mind-map style test cases for review. |
| `test:report` | `testpilot report [name]` | Generate execution statistics from Excel results. |
| `test:archive` | `testpilot archive [name]` | Archive the full test artifact chain for traceability. |

Recommended order:

1. `test:new` creates `testpilot/changes/<name>/proposal.md`.
2. `test:analysis` creates `requirements-analysis.md`.
3. `test:points` creates `specs/testpoints.md`.
4. `test:excel` creates `artifacts/<name>_cases.xlsx`.
5. `test:mind` creates `artifacts/<name>_cases.xmind`.
6. `test:report` creates `report.md` after Excel execution results are filled.
7. `test:archive` moves the change to `testpilot/changes/archive/<date>-<name>/`.

Important: `test:*` entries are Agent workflow labels, not shell commands. Use `testpilot <subcommand>` in the terminal.
<!-- END TESTPILOT AGENT WORKFLOW -->
